request.message: compare 2 acne treatment options — include timeframes like 'week 4 — visible change' in the details

response_body.response:
I need to know: is your acne inflammatory (red, tender bumps) or comedonal (blackheads, whiteheads), and is your skin oily, dry, or combo? that'll tell me which two options make sense to compare.

the evidence I have covers azelaic acid + retinoid as a standard acne ladder, plus anti-inflammatory diet for rosacea-adjacent acne. but I want to make sure I'm comparing the right two protocols for your skin type before I build the block.

products.name: The Ordinary Azelaic Acid Suspension 10%
products.description: Azelaic acid for redness, PIH, and mild rosacea — pregnancy-safe.

products.name: Differin Adapalene Gel 0.1%
products.description: OTC retinoid (adapalene 0.1%) — first-line for comedonal acne.
